back_translation: And the Lord said to Moses: Speak to the priests, the sons of Aaron, and say to them: For a dead person he shall not defile himself among his people,
except for his close relative near to him—for his mother, for his father, for his son, for his daughter, and for his brother,
and for his virgin sister who is near to him, who has not had a husband—for her he may defile himself.
He shall not defile himself as a husband among his people, to profane himself.
They shall not make bald spots on their heads, and the edges of their beards they shall not shave, and in their flesh they shall not make a cut.
Holy they shall be to their God, and they shall not profane the name of their God. For the fire offerings of the Lord, the bread of their God, they present, and they shall be holy.
A prostitute woman or one defiled they shall not take, and a woman divorced from her husband they shall not take, for he is holy to his God.
And you shall sanctify him, for the bread of your God he presents. Holy he shall be to you, for holy am I, the Lord, who sanctifies you.
And the daughter of a priest, if she profanes herself by prostituting—she profanes her father; in fire she shall be burned.

And the priest who is greatest from his brothers, on whose head the anointing oil was poured, and whose hand was filled to wear the garments—his head he shall not let loose, and his garments he shall not tear.
And to any dead souls he shall not come; for his father and for his mother he shall not defile himself.
And from the sanctuary he shall not go out, and he shall not profane the sanctuary of his God, for the consecration of the anointing oil of his God is upon him. I am the Lord.
And he—a woman in her virginity he shall take.
A widow and a divorced woman and one defiled, a prostitute—these he shall not take, but a virgin from his own people he shall take as a wife.
And he shall not profane his offspring among his people, for I am the Lord who sanctifies him.
And the Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
Speak to Aaron, saying: A man from your offspring throughout their generations who has a defect in him shall not approach to offer the bread of his God.
For any man who has a defect shall not approach—a blind man, or lame, or with a disfigured face, or deformed,
or a man who has in him a broken foot, or a broken hand,
or a hunchback, or thin, or with an eye defect, or scab, or rash, or with crushed testicles.

Any man who has a defect in him from the offspring of Aaron the priest shall not approach to offer the fire offerings of the Lord. A defect is in him; the bread of his God he shall not approach to offer.
The bread of his God, from the holy of holies and from the holy things he may eat.
Only to the veil he shall not come, and to the altar he shall not approach, because a defect is in him; and he shall not profane My sanctuaries, for I am the Lord who sanctifies them.
And Moses spoke to Aaron and to his sons and to all the sons of Israel.
